name: node-validate-npm-package-name
title: node-validate-npm-package-name
summary: Checks if a string is a valid npm package name
description:
Checks if a string is a valid npm package name
This module can determine if a string is valid to be used a npm module name.
This module will show conformance to old naming rules and new naming rules
separately.
.
Node.js is an event-based server-side JavaScript engine.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use node-validate-npm-package-name because Checks if a string is a valid npm package name.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use node-validate-npm-package-name as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair node-validate-npm-package-name with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `node-validate-npm-package-name --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
